summary.result: Boots through the blue transition rectangle into live outdoor gameplay. Stable enough as a gameplay render reference, with the known upper/lower composition issue still visible around transition/start.
Briefly reaches a first-person Party render, then collapses to the blue rectangle and black. This separates Party from City/The End: it can enter the stage before the render path dies.
Boots into live first-person Labs rendering for the sampled window. Encoder/door freeze is not covered by this direct no-input probe.
Boots into Hotel geometry but alternates with blown-out sky/white-blue frames. This reproduces the prism class as live render-state corruption, not a simple load failure.
Gets past the transition rectangle into a stable-looking rendered scene for the sampled window. Prior Tower freeze is likely tied to intro/menu transition flow rather than basic stage load.
Shows only the blue transition rectangle followed by black. No useful world render was captured.
Gets past the transition rectangle into a very dark stage render. Prior Boat intro freeze may be transition/camera-flow related rather than a total stage-load failure.
Boots into live Volcano gameplay but produces the same prism/blown-out frame family as Hotel.
Matches City: blue transition rectangle followed by black, with no useful world render captured.

implication: The failures are not one uniform 'stage too large' problem. Direct boot shows several reported problem stages can load enough to render gameplay, while City/The End die before useful world rendering and Hotel/Volcano corrupt during live rendering. The next patch pass should target shared render/camera/VI state transitions and framebuffer/zbuffer ownership rather than menu text or another broad manual playthrough.
